title: festuca varia subsp. pumila
intro: Festuca varia subsp. pumila, also known as alpine fescue, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae, commonly found in alpine and subalpine regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of alpine fescue include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Alpine fescue can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and effectiveness.
